data.content:
I can see the project — and there's a failing test (`CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m`).

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so it re-runs tests automatically on every file save:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` to get hot reload for their tests. Let me start that up for them. I can see there's a failing test - the express delivery test expects 15.00 but the method returns 10.00.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can iterate.

data.content: The watcher is running but there's a build error — missing `using System;`. Let me fix that so the tests can actually compile and run:
data.reasoningText: There's a build error — `ArgumentException` needs `System` namespace. Let me fix that first, then the watch will pick it up. Actually, the user asked me to set up hot reload, not fix the tests. But there's a build error preventing the tests from running at all. Let me fix the build error so the watch loop works, then the user can iterate on the failing test.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but gets `10.00`

The watcher will automatically rebuild and re-run tests whenever you save a file. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` (line 23) from `10.00m` to `15.00m`. Want me to fix it?
